headline: Record 25.5% efficiency for CIGS-perovskite tandem solar cell
summary_80w: Researchers from HZB and Humboldt-Universität in Berlin have achieved a record 25.5% conversion efficiency in a CIGS-perovskite tandem PV cell.

headline: Europe goes big: BESS projects totalling 11GWh launched by IPPs BW ESS, Greenvolt and Giga Storage
summary_80w: Three massive BESS projects have been launched in Europe: BW ESS has broken ground on a 1GW/5.7GWh system in Germany, while Greenvolt and Giga Storage have enlisted suppliers (BYD and Tesla) for 2.4GWh and 2.8GWh projects in Poland and Belgium.

headline: Energy storage essential to ASEAN nations’ knowledge-based economy transition and uncoupling from volatile fossil fuel costs
summary_80w: The rising costs of coal and gas mean that the adoption of renewable energy is a “survival issue” for countries in Southeast Asia, and energy storage is critical to the transition.

headline: Wärtsilä completes 300MWh Bungama BESS for Revera Energy in South Australia
summary_80w: Wärtsilä has brought the 150MW/300MWh Bungama BESS online in South Australia, for owner and operator Revera Energy.

headline: Samsung C&T submits 600MWh Comet Park BESS to Australia’s EPBC Act
summary_80w: Samsung C&T Renewable Energy Australia has submitted the 600MWh Comet Park BESS for federal environmental assessment under the EPBC Act.

headline: Australian AI developer locks in 1.5GWh of battery energy storage under 12-year agreement
summary_80w: Australian AI infrastructure developer Firmus Technologies has signed a 12-year wholesale energy supply agreement with Gunvor Group, including 1.5GWh of battery storage by 2032.

headline: First Solar faces lawsuit over ‘misleading’ claims about impact of US tariffs
summary_80w: First Solar is facing a class action lawsuit from shareholders over its response to US tariff policy and alleged “misleading” statements about its resilience to the shifting policy landscape.

headline: EnBW, Elements Green and VPI launch construction, enlist suppliers for 2.5GWh of BESS in Germany
summary_80w: EnBW and VPI have launched construction on large-scale BESS projects in Germany, while Elements Green and Eku Energy have agreed supply and acquisition deals respectively for separate 400MW/1,600MWh projects.

headline: Iberdrola commissions Italy’s largest solar PV project
summary_80w: Spanish utility Iberdrola has commissioned a 243MW solar PV plant in Italy, the country’s largest in operation.

headline: Decision-making delays remain an obstacle to C&I deployment
summary_80w: The delays caused by the decision-making process required to deploy C&I solar projects are a key obstacle to installing C&I projects.

headline: Maxeon, Hanwha file ‘joint motion’ to dismiss 2024 patent infringement lawsuit
summary_80w: Maxeon and Hanwha have agreed to dismiss a court case in which Maxeon accused Hanwha of patent infringement pertaining to TOPCon technology.

headline: Mexico targets 12GW of new solar PV additions by 2030
summary_80w: The government of Mexico has targeted to install 22GW of new renewables by 2030, of which 12GW will be from solar PV.

headline: Avangrid to build 82MWh Oregon BESS project
summary_80w: Spanish utility Iberdrola's subsidiary Avangrid has announced plans to construct a 41MW/82MWh BESS facility in Gilliam County, Oregon.

headline: EU Roundup: Axpo and e-Storage partner in Italy, RES secures Nordic asset management deal, R.Power sells Poland BESS to Engie
summary_80w: Axpo and e-Storage have partnered on a BESS in southern Italy, RES Group has signed a full-scope battery asset management agreement in Sweden, while R.Power has agreed to sell a Poland BESS project to Engie.

headline: CATL, CALB among backers of new China BESS industry payment and contracting initiative
summary_80w: On 29 June, the China Energy Storage Alliance (CNESA) and the China Automotive Battery Industry Innovation Alliance jointly released the 'Initiative on Standardising Supplier Payment Practices for Power and Stationary Storage Battery Enterprises.'

headline: Energy North submits 1GW off-grid hyperscale data centre with 16GWh of battery storage to Australia’s EPBC Act
summary_80w: Energy North has submitted a proposed 1GW hyperscale data centre campus with a co-located 16GWh BESS to Australia's EPBC Act.

headline: Neoen Australia begins construction on 963MWh battery storage system at 440MWp solar PV plant
summary_80w: French independent power producer (IPP) Neoen Australia has begun construction of a 215MW/963MWh battery energy storage system (BESS) at its 440MWp Culcairn Solar Farm in New South Wales, Australia.

headline: Akaysha Energy’s 1,660MWh Orana BESS reaches commercial operations in Australia
summary_80w: BlackRock-backed developer Akaysha Energy has announced that its 1,660MWh Orana BESS has reached commercial operations in Australia.

headline: Oman launches 1GW solar-plus-storage and 500MW solar PV tender
summary_80w: Nama Power and Water Procurement has launched a tender for two utility-scale solar projects in Oman with a combined capacity of 1.5GW.

headline: RWE, PPC start operations at 930MW Greece PV portfolio in former lignite mine
summary_80w: German energy firm RWE and Greek power supplier PPC have completed construction on a 930MW portfolio of solar PV projects in northern Greece.

headline: Interest in innovation tender decreases as Germany awards 482MW solar-plus-storage
summary_80w: The German Federal Network Agency, Bundesnetzagentur, has awarded 482MW of solar-plus-storage in its latest innovation tender.

headline: SAEL breaks ground on 5GW cell, 5GW module manufacturing facility in Uttar Pradesh
summary_80w: SAEL Industries has broken ground on a 10GW integrated solar manufacturing facility in Jewar, Uttar Pradesh.

headline: Federal permitting red tape puts 30% of US solar pipeline at risk – Wood Mackenzie
summary_80w: Over US$121 billion of investment across 92GW of renewables projects in the US is at risk from federal scrutiny, according to Wood Mackenzie.

headline: Research points to ‘recyclability of solar panel components’ in Australia
summary_80w: Developing more robust PV module recycling methods in Australia could be of economic benefit to the companies involved in recycling.

headline: LONGi unveils mobile off-grid PV unit, seals 80MW Greece module order
summary_80w: Chinese PV manufacturer LONGi has unveiled a new containerised solar solution designed for remote off-grid industrial-scale applications.

headline: EBOS steps into the spotlight
summary_80w: eBOS hardware, long overlooked in PV design, is now central to solar project cost optimisation as technologies advance, writes Shreeyashi Ojha.

headline: EDF agrees to sell North American renewables business to KKR
summary_80w: French utility EDF has agreed to sell its renewable energy business in the US and Canada to private equity firm KKR.

headline: Pakistan added 27GW of distributed solar in 2023-25 as consumers move off-grid
summary_80w: Pakistan installed 27GW of distributed solar in the last two years, prompting a 21% surge in electricity demand, according to a new report from UK-based energy think tank Ember.

headline: Runergy launches TOPCon module with 26.9% cell conversion efficiency
summary_80w: Runergy has launched the third generation of its TOPCon solar modules, which have a conversion efficiency of 26.9%.

headline: Australia: AEMO’s 2026 ISP calls for nearly 120GW of utility-scale wind and solar by 2050
summary_80w: Australia needs to build nearly 120GW of utility-scale wind and solar by 2050, approximately five times the current level, says AEMO.

headline: Flow batteries selected for microgrid deployments in Maldives, Paskenta Tribe lands in California
summary_80w: Two recent microgrid projects highlight the expanding role of flow batteries, with Quino Energy deploying organic flow batteries in the Maldives and the Paskenta Band of Nomlaki Indians integrating zinc hybrid cathode storage in Northern California.

headline: EU tripartite agreement targets 30-35GW of storage deployments by 2028
summary_80w: The European Union (EU) has announced a tripartite agreement to accelerate energy storage deployments in the next two years.

headline: Envision to supply 660MWh of battery storage for South Africa’s ‘largest private hybrid power project’
summary_80w: Envision has signed a 660MWh BESS supply agreement with SOLA Group and construction company WBHO in South Africa's Free State.

headline: AGL submits 50MW/100MWh Awaba BESS to Australia’s EPBC Act
summary_80w: Australian utility AGL Energy has submitted a 100MWh BESS in New South Wales for federal environmental assessment under the EPBC Act.

headline: ‘Decisions look obvious after the event. They rarely are in real time’: OptiGrid on South Australia’s battery storage price cap event
summary_80w: OptiGrid's Sahand Karimi examines why state of charge & algorithm quality drove divergent outcomes during SA1's AU$20,300/MWh price cap on 21 June.

headline: Waaree expands EPC footprint through US$129 million acquisition
summary_80w: Waaree Renewable Technologies has acquired a 55% stake in Associated Power Structures for INR12.25 billion (US$129 million).

headline: Intersolar 2026: Key takeaways from a week with Europe’s solar industry
summary_80w: Europe’s solar industry seemed a little downbeat as it trudged to Munich for Intersolar Europe 2026 this week. Under the unforgiving June heat, PV Tech took the temperature of the industry.

headline: VinEnergo, SunAsia to develop 422MWp floating solar portfolio in Philippines
summary_80w: VinEnergo and SunAsia Energy have partnered to develop 422MWp of floating solar projects in the Philippines.

headline: Inverters could be Nextpower’s next steps after Prevalon, Zimmermann deals
summary_80w: PV Tech spoke with Bloomberg Intelligence about Nextpower's acquisition of Zimmermann and how this was a logical next step.

headline: Intersolar Europe 2026: Astronergy showcases ASTRO N7s 3.0 residential PV module
summary_80w: Chinese solar manufacturer Astronergy has launched ASTRO N7s 3.0, its latest residential solar module, at Intersolar Europe 2026.

headline: Enlight secures financing for 1.2GW/4GWh Arizona solar and storage ‘Complex’
summary_80w: IPP Enlight Renewable Energy has announced that its US subsidiary Clēnera Holdings has entered into a debt financing framework agreement for the CO Bar Complex in Arizona, US.

headline: UK’s Ofgem goes big on LDES cap-and-floor window 1; 18-hour lithium-ion among winners
summary_80w: UK energy regulator Ofgem has shortlisted 16 projects for the first ever long-duration energy storage (LDES) cap-and-floor scheme, totalling 7.6GW of capacity ranging from 8- to 22-hour durations.

headline: Energy Dome’s second CO2 Battery project with Google will secure carbon-free energy for tech giant while easing grid congestion in Ireland
summary_80w: Energy Dome has signed its second low-carbon energy supply contract with Google.

headline: Asian Development Bank supports 500MWh grid-forming battery energy storage project in Cambodia
summary_80w: ADB has approved a Royal Government of Cambodia application for financial support for a 250MW/500MWh battery storage project.

headline: Goldwind Capital submits 1GWh wind-plus-storage site to Australia’s EPBC Act
summary_80w: Goldwind Capital has submitted the Milpulling Wind Farm, a 1GWh wind-plus-storage site for assessment under Australia's EPBC Act.

headline: Trends to look out for at Energy Storage Summit Asia 2026
summary_80w: Energy Storage Summit Asia 2026 convenes in Bangkok as Vietnam's tariff reform, Cambodia's grid milestone, and AI demand reshape the region.

headline: Pathfinder secures €100 million to finance 400MW solar, 200MW storage portfolio in the UK
summary_80w: Pathfinder Clean Energy (PACE) has secured a €100 million investment to support its deployment of a solar and BESS portfolio in the UK.

headline: Galileo advances 630MW French renewable energy pipeline
summary_80w: Galileo is advancing a 630MW renewables pipeline in France, with permitting applications for 11 projects due in the coming months.

headline: R.Power secures finance for 75MWp Romanian PV portfolio
summary_80w: R.Power has secured a €41.6 million (US$47.3 million) project finance facility for four solar projects in Romania with a combined capacity of approximately 75MWp.

headline: NLC India, OREDA sign JV to develop 1GW of renewable energy in Odisha
summary_80w: NLC India and OREDA have signed a joint venture agreement to develop 1,000MW of renewable energy capacity in Odisha.

headline: ITRPV 2026: Solar industry maintains historic learning curve despite market turbulence
summary_80w: The annual ITRPV report was published this week, offering a snapshot of the latest technological trends shaping the industry.

headline: US ROUND-UP: Vesper, rPlus and Matrix advance PV projects across the country
summary_80w: US developers Vesper Energy, rPlus Energies and Matrix Renewables have announced a number of project advancements in the US this week.

headline: Tesla, Sunrun, Renew Home announce 16GW VPP framework, focus on PJM
summary_80w: US residential solar and energy storage installer Sunrun, energy management platform Renew Home, and Tesla have announced an agreement to deliver more than 16GW of flexible energy capacity to US hyperscalers and utilities.

headline: US ROUNDUP: e-Storage, Spearmint Energy, and OCI Energy advance BESS projects in Michigan and Texas
summary_80w: In this US news roundup, e-Storage, Spearmint Energy, and OCI Energy advance battery energy storage system (BESS) projects and financing in Michigan and Texas.

headline: Sodium-ion will reach cost parity with LFP ‘in two to three years’, BESS firm says
summary_80w: Sodium-ion BESS startup Moonwatt expects the battery chemistry to reach price cost parity with lithium iron phosphate (LFP) in the next two or three years, according to its CCO, Valentin Rota.

headline: Nextpower and Prevalon CEOs discuss acquisition background and future strategy
summary_80w: ESN Premium speaks with Nextpower CEO Dan Shugar and Prevalon’s Tom Cornell about the solar PV solutions company’s (re)entry into the energy storage space.

headline: ees Europe 2026: Grid-forming PCS, DC-coupling, C&I and data centre solutions among product launches and market debuts
summary_80w: With ees Europe taking place in Munich, Germany, this week alongside Intersolar Europe and Smarter E, Energy-Storage.news takes a look at some expo highlights.

headline: Australia: AEMO calls for 35GW of short and medium-duration storage plus 5GW of long-duration storage by 2050
summary_80w: AEMO has set out a storage requirement of almost 50GW in its latest 2026 Integrated System Plan (ISP), split between 35GW of short and medium-duration battery storage for daily firming and 5GW of long-duration energy storage (LDES) for seasonal reliability.

headline: Germany’s battery energy storage market is entering a new phase of maturity
summary_80w: Germany's policymakers, regulators and energy storage industry must carefully consider the issues of grid connections and grid fees, writes Thomas Antonioli of developer Terra One.

headline: Toyo Solar sells shares to finance HJT cell manufacturing in the US
summary_80w: Toyo Solar has raised around US$50 million to finance the expansion of the company’s cell manufacturing work in Houston, Texas.

headline: Comstock Metals to establish solar panel recycling and logistics hub in Ohio
summary_80w: Comstock Metals in collaboration with JobsOhio and OhioSE will establish an industrial-scale solar panel recycling plant in Cambridge, Ohio.

headline: Intersolar Europe 2026: Domestic solar manufacturing won’t be a ‘disruptor’, Fronius pushes EU-made message
summary_80w: Europe will not be able to produce a “disruption” in the solar manufacturing industry, according to Peter Fath, CEO of RCT Solutions.

headline: Intersolar Europe 2026: GCL-SI launches GPC 3.0 all-black back-contact module
summary_80w: GCL System Integration Technology (GCL-SI) has set back-contact (BC) cell technology as the core pillar of its next-generation PV roadmap.

headline: Intersolar Europe 2026: Sungrow launches 125kW C&I string inverter
summary_80w: Sungrow has launched its SG125CX-P3 string inverter, designed for use in the C&I sector, at the ongoing Intersolar Europe trade show.

headline: China issues renewables consumption rules set to fuel new growth cycle for solar PV and energy storage
summary_80w: Nationwide mandatory consumption obligations in China will underpin long-term growth in solar PV and wind installed capacity.

headline: US manufacturers file AD/CVD request against Hanwha and other cell makers in South Korea
summary_80w: A new anti-circumvention inquiry request has been filed with the US Department of Commerce against Hanwha and other solar cell producers regarding the import of solar cells from South Korea to the US.

headline: Intersolar Europe 2026: LONGi launches Hi-MO 9 Prime modules, ‘scenario-based’ variants
summary_80w: LONGi has launched its Hi-MO 9 Prime series of modules at Intersolar Europe 2026, which have a conversion efficiency of 25.2%.

headline: FER X brings certainty to Italy’s renewables market, but deployment hurdles remain
summary_80w: Patrizio Donati, co-founder, Terrawatt, talks about Italy's €23 billion FER X scheme and its impact on renewable energy deployment.

headline: How LONGi is driving true technological value
summary_80w: LONGi's Louis Liu discusses the company's evolution from module supplier into an integrated clean energy systems partner.

headline: Dominion Energy launches long-duration energy storage RFI in Virginia
summary_80w: US utility Dominion Energy Virginia (DEV) is issuing a request for information (RFI) for information on long-duration energy storage (LDES) technologies.

headline: EU funding ban on Chinese inverters drives BESS industry towards optionality, supply chain resiliency
summary_80w: ESN Premium heard from executives at battery energy storage system (BESS) firms Rept and Fluence on the EU funding ban on Chinese inverters in solar and BESS projects.

headline: US energy storage installations will quadruple by 2031, Wood Mackenzie forecasts
summary_80w: Market research firm Wood Mackenzie predicted that the US energy storage market will almost quadruple over the next six years.

headline: Grid-forming technology explored and explained: Download our white paper, with Kehua Digital Energy & TÜV Rheinland
summary_80w: ‘Enhancing System Stability through Grid-Forming Energy Storage Technologies,’ has been launched by Energy-Storage.news, sponsor Kehua Digital Energy and TÜV Rheinland.

headline: NatPower, Tesla sign 25GWh Megapack supply and execution agreement for Italy and UK BESS projects
summary_80w: NatPower and Tesla have signed a multi-year supply and execution agreement covering more than 25GWh of BESS across Italy and the UK.

headline: Australia awards 4.2GW/16.1GWh of battery storage under Capacity Investment Scheme Tender 8
summary_80w: The Australian government has announced the results of Capacity Investment Scheme (CIS) Tender 8, selecting 15 battery energy storage projects across the National Electricity Market (NEM), totalling 4.2GW/16.1GWh.

headline: rPlus Energies’ 1,600MWh Utah solar-plus-storage project begins commercial operation
summary_80w: Renewables developer rPlus Energies has announced start of commercial operations for the 400MW/1,600MWh Green River Energy Centre in Emery County, Utah, US.

headline: Meridian Energy’s 622MW Waiinu hybrid project enters New Zealand fast-track consenting pathway
summary_80w: Meridian's Waiinu Energy Park has been referred for consideration under New Zealand's Fast-track Approvals Act.

headline: Intersolar Europe 2026: SMA Solar launches grid-forming inverters and management software
summary_80w: SMA Solar has launched a suite of products at Intersolar Europe 2026, including grid-forming inverters and power plant management software.

headline: I Squared Capital, GS E&C JV to develop 1.5GW solar PV, BESS in South Korea
summary_80w: Infrastructure investment manager I Squared Capital has signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Korean construction company GS E&C to develop renewables in the country.

headline: RENA Technologies to supply 6GW TOPCon solar cell equipment to India’s Emmvee Energy
summary_80w: German solar PV equipment manufacturer RENA Technologies has signed a supply agreement for solar cell production equipment with Indian manufacturer Emmvee Energy.

headline: Intersolar Europe 2026: Aiko launches ABC modules for utility-scale and residential sectors
summary_80w: Aiko has launched the fourth generation of its Infinite Ultra ABC modules and the new Z series range of modules at Intersolar Europe 2026.

headline: Intersolar Europe 2026: Global Solar Council launches AgriPV Task Force
summary_80w: Trade association the Global Solar Council (GSC) has launched an agrivoltaics (agriPV) task force aimed at accelerating solar PV’s growth globally.

headline: Meta, Sabanci sign PPA for Texas PV portfolio
summary_80w: Sabanci Renewables has signed a PPA with Meta for a portfolio of solar PV projects currently under development in the state of Texas.

headline: Australian Centre for Advanced Photovoltaics ranked first globally for solar research
summary_80w: Australia's ACAP was ranked first globally for photovoltaics research quality in 2025 for the second consecutive year.

headline: Hong Kong energy storage stock rally gains new contenders: Cubenergy files for IPO, Anker Innovation clears listing hearing
summary_80w: The trend for mainland China-listed energy storage firms to seek secondary listings on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange shows no sign of abating, writes Carrie Xiao.

headline: ZincFive bets on revenue track record in US$752 million SPAC merger after 2021 cohort struggles
summary_80w: ZincFive, a manufacturer of nickel-zinc battery systems for data centre and AI infrastructure applications, announced on 11 June that it will become publicly traded through a merger with special purpose acquisition company (SPAC) Spark I Acquisition Corporation.

headline: CATL: System- and cell-level advances driving sodium-ion energy density improvements
summary_80w: Executives from lithium-ion battery and BESS manufacturer CATL discussed sodium-ion technology at Smarter E Europe today.

headline: REV Renewables and LS Power commission California’s first 8-hour duration lithium-ion BESS
summary_80w: LS Power-owned REV Renewables and US development and investment company LS Power have commissioned the Tumbleweed Energy Storage facility in Kern County, California, US.

headline: EU Roundup: Optimisation and offtake for large-scale BESS with Flower and ENGIE in Germany; Entrix and DRI in Poland
summary_80w: Flower and ENGIE sign a 7-year virtual toll for 126MW in Germany, while Entrix wins optimisation rights for DRI's 133MW Polish BESS.

headline: CATL claims purpose-built sodium-ion BESS can help solve ‘uncertainties’ facing industry
summary_80w: CATL held the global launch of its large-scale sodium-ion BESS yesterday, unveiling a 30MWh+ module which it will ship outside China from next year.

headline: ‘Policy certainty takes priority’ in APAC battery storage investment decisions, says Banpu NEXT
summary_80w: In this interview with Energy-Storage.news, Colin Koon Peng Ho of Banpu NEXT explains why policy certainty now outweighs cost in APAC battery storage investment decisions, and discusses regulatory barriers, supply chain localisation and the most attractive BESS opportunities.

headline: Defying the PV industry downturn: how PV-storage companies survive and thrive globally
summary_80w: PV Tech spoke with Hanersun's chairman about the company's PV-storage strategy, global expansion and the Chinese market outlook.

headline: Canadian Solar launches TOPCon 3.0 module with 24.8% conversion efficiency
summary_80w: Canadian Solar has announced its TOPCon 3.0 module, which has a power output of 670W and a conversion efficiency of 24.8%.

headline: Lego Group begins construction of 116MW solar park in Denmark
summary_80w: The Lego Group has started construction of a 116MW solar park in Billund, which is expected to become its “largest solar project to date.”

headline: CIM Group closes construction financing at California 250MW/600MWh solar-plus-storage project
summary_80w: Energy platform Permanent Power Company has secured US$600 million in construction financing for a solar-plus-storage project in California, US.

headline: There is now more installed energy storage capacity than nuclear in Europe, yet ‘no country has reached its potential’
summary_80w: As of the second quarter of this year, installed energy storage capacity in Europe overtook nuclear, according to trade association Energy Storage Europe’s new report.

headline: Multi-day energy storage startup Ore Energy signs 1GWh iron-air battery deal with Dutch utility
summary_80w: Startup Ore Energy has signed an agreement with a utility in the Netherlands to deploy up to 1,000MWh of multi-day-duration iron-air batteries.

headline: South Australia hits AU$20,300/MWh price cap twice in one evening as battery storage fleet captures AU$324,000
summary_80w: South Australia's SA1 trading region reached the NEM AU$20,300/MWh price cap twice during the evening of 21 June.

headline: US BESS integrator FlexGen, LDES tech provider Eos enter European market with first deals
summary_80w: FlexGen and Eos Energy Enterprises, two US energy storage sector companies with different specialisations, have entered the European market.

headline: European utilities’ association Eurelectric: Long-duration energy storage an increasingly viable flexibility option
summary_80w: Long-duration energy storage (LDES) technologies are becoming increasingly viable options to add flexibility to the European electricity network, according to a new report.
